---
author:
  - name: Konrad Grzanek
    affiliationId: 1
  - name: Andrzej Cader
    affiliationId: 1
affiliation:
  - id: 1
    name: ["IT Institute, University of Social Sciences",
           "9 Sienkiewicza St., 90-113 Lodz, Poland"]
    emails: ["kgrzanek@spoleczna.pl", "acader@spoleczna.pl"]
title: "A Capitalized Title of My Paper in JACSM"
abstract: >
  Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Vestibulum rutrum
  maximus tortor, vel ornare massa pretium eu. Mauris sit amet turpis id nunc
  sagittis posuere in a lacus. Sed quis enim eget justo gravida sollicitudin quis
  eget diam. Quisque id tellus eu orci mollis sagittis aliquam ut dolor. Nam nec
  ex ut orci eleifend malesuada in lobortis eros. Nulla non magna ante. Phasellus
  congue viverra fringilla.
keywords: keywords, not capitalized, "and so on"
cmyk: false
mathptmx: false
output:
  rticles::jacsm_article:
    keep_tex: true
---

# Introduction

This template demonstrates some of the basic latex you'll need to know to
create a JACSM article.

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Vestibulum rutrum
maximus tortor, vel ornare massa pretium eu. Mauris sit amet turpis id nunc
sagittis posuere in a lacus. Sed quis enim eget justo gravida sollicitudin quis
eget diam. Quisque id tellus eu orci mollis sagittis aliquam ut dolor. Nam nec
ex ut orci eleifend malesuada in lobortis eros. Nulla non magna ante. Phasellus
congue viverra fringilla.

## Also add some subsection

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Vestibulum rutrum
maximus tortor, vel ornare massa pretium eu. Mauris sit amet turpis id nunc
sagittis posuere in a lacus.

# Solution

Suspendisse quam nisl, laoreet ut vehicula a, eleifend vel massa. Morbi
faucibus sem varius placerat lacinia.

# R code

Can be inserted in regular R markdown blocks.

```{r}
x <- seq(from = -1, to = 1, length.out = 100)
y <- 2 * x + rnorm(100, sd = 0.5)
summary(data.frame(x, y))
```

# Plots

```{r echo=TRUE, fig.cap="A simple plot", out.width="75%"}
plot(x, y)
```

# Formulas and Equations

Formulas may be embedded in the text like $X \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu,\,\sigma^{2})$,
or placed on a separate line:

$$y = Ax + b$$

\begin{thebibliography}{4}

\bibitem{example} Author A., Author B.: Title of the paper. Journal Name,
  Vol. 1, No. 1, pp. 1--10 (2020)

\end{thebibliography}
